summary: Machine-id-keyed encrypted payload executed via new Function() on require
details: On require() of the package, index.js silently loads lib/perf.js inside a swallowed try/catch (`try { require('./lib/perf'); } catch (_) {}`). lib/perf.js reads /etc/machine-id, uses its hex value as an AES-128-CBC key with a hardcoded IV to decrypt a ~4 KB embedded hex ciphertext, and executes the cleartext through `new Function(r)()`. The module exports nothing and hides any error, concealing the side effect. The package advertises itself as a TypeScript naming-convention linter (exports checkName/lintProject/getConfig); lib/perf.js performs no perf work and is not referenced elsewhere — its filename is cover-story naming. Keying decryption to /etc/machine-id is a victim-targeting mechanism: the payload only runs on machines whose machine-id the attacker already knows, which both narrows the attack to specific installers and prevents researchers without the right machine-id from recovering the payload. Any installer requiring this package on a targeted host gets arbitrary attacker-controlled code executed in their Node process at import time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
